subject: Robot config seggregation
commit_message:
Robot config seggregation

Issue-ID: OOM-560

Change-Id: Iee9c7c78c6eb53b14b31d6e31f1539040daa88ba
Signed-off-by: vaibhav_16dec <vaibhav.chopra@amdocs.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(16 comments)

lot of changes are required.

openstack/onap parameter should be replaced using tpl. check mso code for reference.

some config map related changes also required.

Thanks
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

(6 comments)

Have Uploaded new patch handling the Review comments.

Patch Set 2:

(5 comments)

Hi Borislav,

Could you please review this task.

BR/
VC

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-2

(2 comments)

Much of the robot config needs to be dynamically injected which I don't see happening with this solution.  Sure the pod will launch but it won't be of any use to someone trying to use it.
For example:
This needs to take my openstack project password, encrypt it using a specific key found in SO's git repo and then store it in robots configuration.
This hardcod it to something that won't work:
openStackEncryptedPassword: "c124921a3a0efbe579782cde8227681e"

Patch Set 2:

I suggest studying how robot on the VM boots up to understand one way of doing it...Then try to apply some helm best practices to achieve something similar or better.
Uploaded patch set 3.

As discussed with Mike, All the Environment parameters also need to be standardized and it could be under common templates aswell so to track same, I have created below JIRA:-

https://jira.onap.org/browse/OOM-763
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

I have started working over Standardization, but this task need to be merged first before going ahead of merging same.
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 3:

Hi Borislav and Alexis

Post resolving conflict and checking out this task, I am getting below error
Steps I did:-
1) Clone Master
2) Check out this task
3) Pull out the Changes of run namespace
4) Resolved the Robot File conflicts (Which were not resolved by the above)

Now Post above steps while committing its not allowing me to do:-
root@k8s-1:/vaibhav/Robot_Patch8/oom# git rebase --continue
kubernetes/config/docker/init/src/config/robot/eteshare/config/vm_properties.py: needs merge


Am I doing something wrong ? Do I need to incorporate all the changes of run namespace. (I would prefer then Opening a New task)

Could you please suggest.
Uploaded patch set 4.
